infobox.value: To educate filmmakers and honor the heritage of the [history of cinema in the United States](/wiki/History_of_cinema_in_the_United_States "History of cinema in the United States")

* [Bob Gazzale](/wiki/Bob_Gazzale "Bob Gazzale") (president and CEO) * [Kathleen Kennedy](/wiki/Kathleen_Kennedy_(producer) "Kathleen Kennedy (producer)") (chair, Board of Trustees) * [Robert A. Daly](/wiki/Robert_A._Daly "Robert A. Daly") (chair, board of directors)

(**AFI**) is an American nonprofit [film](/wiki/Film "Film") organization that educates filmmakers and honors the heritage of the [motion picture arts in the United States](/wiki/History_of_cinema_in_the_United_States "History of cinema in the United States"). AFI is supported by private funding and public membership fees.

The **American Film Institute** (**AFI**) is an American nonprofit [film](/wiki/Film "Film") organization that educates filmmakers and honors the heritage of the [motion picture arts in the United States](/wiki/History_of_cinema_in_the_United_States "History of cinema in the United States"). AFI is supported by private funding and public membership fees.

## Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Leadership")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/43/2_2011-09-29_WarnerBldg_Facade_SP-Pano1.jpg/250px-2_2011-09-29_WarnerBldg_Facade_SP-Pano1.jpg)](/wiki/File:2_2011-09-29_WarnerBldg_Facade_SP-Pano1.jpg)

The historic [Spanish Colonial Revival](/wiki/Spanish_Colonial_Revival "Spanish Colonial Revival") style AFI campus in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), in the [Los Feliz](/wiki/Los_Feliz,_Los_Angeles "Los Feliz, Los Angeles") district of L.A.

The institute is composed of leaders from the film, entertainment, business, and academic communities. The board of trustees is chaired by [Kathleen Kennedy](/wiki/Kathleen_Kennedy_(producer) "Kathleen Kennedy (producer)") and the board of directors chaired by [Robert A. Daly](/wiki/Robert_A._Daly "Robert A. Daly") guide the organization, which is led by President and CEO, [film historian](/wiki/Film_historian "Film historian") [Bob Gazzale](/wiki/Bob_Gazzale "Bob Gazzale"). Prior leaders were founding director [George Stevens Jr.](/wiki/George_Stevens_Jr. "George Stevens Jr.") (from the organization's inception in 1967 until 1980) and [Jean Picker Firstenberg](/wiki/Jean_Picker_Firstenberg "Jean Picker Firstenberg") (from 1980 to 2007).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The American Film Institute was founded by a 1965 presidential mandate announced in the [Rose Garden](/wiki/White_House_Rose_Garden "White House Rose Garden") of the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House") by [Lyndon B. Johnson](/wiki/Lyndon_B._Johnson "Lyndon B. Johnson")—to establish a national arts organization to preserve the legacy of [American film](/wiki/American_film "American film") heritage, educate the next generation of filmmakers, and honor the artists and their work. Two years later, in 1967, AFI was established, supported by the [National Endowment for the Arts](/wiki/National_Endowment_for_the_Arts "National Endowment for the Arts"), the [Motion Picture Association of America](/wiki/Motion_Picture_Association_of_America "Motion Picture Association of America") and the [Ford Foundation](/wiki/Ford_Foundation "Ford Foundation").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The original 22-member [Board of Trustees](/wiki/Board_of_Trustees "Board of Trustees") included actor [Gregory Peck](/wiki/Gregory_Peck "Gregory Peck") as chairman and actor [Sidney Poitier](/wiki/Sidney_Poitier "Sidney Poitier") as vice-chairman, as well as director [Francis Ford Coppola](/wiki/Francis_Ford_Coppola "Francis Ford Coppola"), film historian [Arthur Schlesinger, Jr.](/wiki/Arthur_Schlesinger,_Jr. "Arthur Schlesinger, Jr."), lobbyist [Jack Valenti](/wiki/Jack_Valenti "Jack Valenti"), and other representatives from the arts and academia.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The institute established a training program for filmmakers known then as the Center for Advanced Film Studies. Also created in the early years were a repertory film exhibition program at the [Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts](/wiki/Kennedy_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts "Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts") and the [AFI Catalog of Feature Films](/wiki/AFI_Catalog_of_Feature_Films "AFI Catalog of Feature Films") — a scholarly source for American film history. The institute moved to its current eight-acre [Hollywood](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles") campus in 1981.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The film training program grew into the [AFI Conservatory](/wiki/AFI_Conservatory "AFI Conservatory"), an accredited graduate school.

AFI moved its presentation of first-run and [auteur films](/wiki/Auteur_films "Auteur films") from the Kennedy Center to the historic [AFI Silver Theatre and Cultural Center](/wiki/AFI_Silver "AFI Silver"), which hosts the AFI DOCS film festival, making AFI the largest nonprofit film exhibitor in the world. AFI educates audiences and recognizes artistic excellence through its awards programs and [10 Top 10 Lists](/wiki/AFI%27s_10_Top_10 "AFI's 10 Top 10").

Further information on the 1984 establishment of the: [National Center for Film and Video Preservation](/wiki/National_Center_for_Film_and_Video_Preservation "National Center for Film and Video Preservation")

In 2017, then-aspiring filmmaker Ilana Bar-Din Giannini claimed that the AFI expelled her after she accused Dezso Magyar of [sexually harassing](/wiki/Sexual_harassment "Sexual harassment") her in the early 1980s.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## List of programs in brief

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: List of programs in brief")]

AFI educational and cultural programs include:

* [American Film Institute Awards](/wiki/American_Film_Institute_Awards "American Film Institute Awards") – an honor celebrating the creative ensembles of the most outstanding motion picture and television programs of the year
* [AFI Catalog of Feature Films](/wiki/AFI_Catalog_of_Feature_Films "AFI Catalog of Feature Films") and AFI Archive – the [written history](/wiki/Recorded_history "Recorded history") of all feature films during the first 100 years of the art form – accessible free online[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [AFI Conservatory](/wiki/AFI_Conservatory "AFI Conservatory") – a film school led by master filmmakers in a graduate-level program[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* AFI [Directing Workshop for Women](/wiki/Directing_Workshop_for_Women "Directing Workshop for Women") – a production-based training program committed to increasing the number of women working professionally in screen directing[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* [AFI Life Achievement Award](/wiki/AFI_Life_Achievement_Award "AFI Life Achievement Award") – a tradition since 1973, a high honor for a career in film[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* [AFI 100 Years... series](/wiki/AFI_100_Years..._series "AFI 100 Years... series") – television events and movie reference lists[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* AFI's two film festivals – AFI Fest in Los Angeles and [AFI Docs](/wiki/AFI_Docs "AFI Docs") in [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C."), and [Silver Spring, Maryland](/wiki/Silver_Spring,_Maryland "Silver Spring, Maryland")[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* [AFI Silver Theatre and Cultural Center](/wiki/AFI_Silver "AFI Silver") – a historic theater with year-round art house, first-run and classic film programming in Silver Spring, Maryland[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* *[American Film](/wiki/American_Film_(magazine) "American Film (magazine)")* – a magazine launched in October 1975 that explores the art of new and historic film classics, now a blog on AFI.com[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## AFI Conservatory

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: AFI Conservatory")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/01/AFI_Conservatory_Fellows_filming_on_the_AFI_campus.jpg/250px-AFI_Conservatory_Fellows_filming_on_the_AFI_campus.jpg)](/wiki/File:AFI_Conservatory_Fellows_filming_on_the_AFI_campus.jpg)

AFI Conservatory Fellows filming on the AFI campus.

In 1969, the institute established the [AFI Conservatory](/wiki/AFI_Conservatory "AFI Conservatory") for Advanced Film Studies at [Greystone, the Doheny Mansion](/wiki/Greystone_Mansion "Greystone Mansion") in [Beverly Hills](/wiki/Beverly_Hills "Beverly Hills"), [California](/wiki/California "California"). The first class included filmmakers [Terrence Malick](/wiki/Terrence_Malick "Terrence Malick"), [Caleb Deschanel](/wiki/Caleb_Deschanel "Caleb Deschanel"), and [Paul Schrader](/wiki/Paul_Schrader "Paul Schrader").[[17]](#cite_note-17) That program grew into the [AFI Conservatory](/wiki/AFI_Conservatory "AFI Conservatory"), an accredited graduate [film school](/wiki/Film_school "Film school") located in the hills above Hollywood, California, providing training in six filmmaking disciplines: [cinematography](/wiki/Cinematography "Cinematography"), [directing](/wiki/Film_directing "Film directing"), [editing](/wiki/Film_editing "Film editing"), [producing](/wiki/Film_production#Production "Film production"), [production design](/wiki/Production_design "Production design"), and [screenwriting](/wiki/Screenwriting "Screenwriting"). Mirroring a professional production environment, Fellows collaborate to make more films than any other graduate level program. Admission to AFI Conservatory is highly selective, with a maximum of 140 graduates per year.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In 2013, [Emmy](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award") and [Oscar](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards")-winning director, producer, and screenwriter [James L. Brooks](/wiki/James_L._Brooks "James L. Brooks") (*[As Good as It Gets](/wiki/As_Good_as_It_Gets "As Good as It Gets")*, *[Broadcast News](/wiki/Broadcast_News_(film) "Broadcast News (film)")*, *[Terms of Endearment](/wiki/Terms_of_Endearment "Terms of Endearment")*) joined as the artistic director of the AFI Conservatory where he provides leadership for the film program.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Brooks' artistic role at the AFI Conservatory has a rich legacy that includes [Daniel Petrie, Jr.](/wiki/Daniel_Petrie,_Jr. "Daniel Petrie, Jr."), [Robert Wise](/wiki/Robert_Wise "Robert Wise"), and [Frank Pierson](/wiki/Frank_Pierson "Frank Pierson"). Award-winning director Bob Mandel served as [dean](/wiki/Dean_(education) "Dean (education)") of the AFI Conservatory for nine years. Jan Schuette took over as dean in 2014 and served until 2017. Film producer [Richard Gladstein](/wiki/Richard_Gladstein "Richard Gladstein") was dean from 2017 until 2019, when [Susan Ruskin](/wiki/Susan_Ruskin "Susan Ruskin") was appointed.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

AFI Conservatory's alumni have careers in film, television and on the web. They have been recognized with all of the major industry awards—Academy Award, Emmy Award, guild awards, and the [Tony Award](/wiki/Tony_Award "Tony Award").[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## AFI Film Festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: AFI Film Festivals")]

AFI operates two film festivals: AFI Fest in Los Angeles, and AFI Docs (formally known as Silverdocs) in [Silver Spring, Maryland](/wiki/Silver_Spring,_Maryland "Silver Spring, Maryland"), and [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.")

### American Film Institute Festival

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: American Film Institute Festival")]

Commonly shortened to **AFI Fest**, it is the American Film Institute's annual celebration of artistic excellence. It is a showcase for the best festival films of the year as selected by AFI and an opportunity for master [filmmakers](/wiki/Filmmakers "Filmmakers") and emerging artists to come together with audiences.[[22]](#cite_note-22) It is the only festival of its stature that is free to the public. The [Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_and_Sciences "Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences") recognizes AFI Fest as a qualifying festival for the Short Films category for the annual [Academy Awards](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards").

The festival was first announced in January 1987 to take the place of [Filmex](/wiki/Filmex "Filmex") in March 1987 with [Ken Wlaschin](/wiki/Ken_Wlaschin "Ken Wlaschin"), former Filmex artistic director, named as director of the new festival. The first festival was funded with a grant of $200,000 from the Interface Group and was to feature 80 films in a non-competitive format with a mix of independent American and foreign films. Its primary venue was the [Los Feliz](/wiki/Los_Feliz "Los Feliz") Theater.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

The festival has paid tribute to numerous influential filmmakers and artists over the years, including [Agnès Varda](/wiki/Agn%C3%A8s_Varda "Agnès Varda"), [Pedro Almodóvar](/wiki/Pedro_Almod%C3%B3var "Pedro Almodóvar") and [David Lynch](/wiki/David_Lynch "David Lynch") as guest artistic directors, and has screened scores of films that have gone on to win Oscar nominations and awards.

The movies selected by AFI are assigned to different sections for the festival; these include Galas/Red Carpet Premieres, Special Screenings, Documentaries, Discovery, and Short Film Competition.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

#### Red Carpet Premieres

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Red Carpet Premieres")]

Formerly named Galas, it is AFI Fest's section for the most highly anticipated films at the festival, presenting selected [feature-length](/wiki/Feature_film "Feature film") movies from world-class [filmmakers](/wiki/Filmmakers "Filmmakers") and artisans.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Although it is a very restrictive selection, usually presenting between three and seven movies at most, many films selected by AFI for this section eventually also earn an [Academy Award Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture") nomination. Examples include [Bradley Cooper](/wiki/Bradley_Cooper "Bradley Cooper")'s *[Maestro](/wiki/Maestro_(2023_film) "Maestro (2023 film)")* (2023),[[26]](#cite_note-26) [Steven Spielberg](/wiki/Steven_Spielberg "Steven Spielberg")'s *[The Fabelmans](/wiki/The_Fabelmans "The Fabelmans")* (2022),[[27]](#cite_note-27) [Will Smith](/wiki/Will_Smith "Will Smith")'s *[King Richard](/wiki/King_Richard_(film) "King Richard (film)")* (2021), [Jane Campion](/wiki/Jane_Campion "Jane Campion")'s *[The Power of the Dog](/wiki/The_Power_of_the_Dog_(film) "The Power of the Dog (film)")* (2021),[[28]](#cite_note-28) [Anthony Hopkins](/wiki/Anthony_Hopkins "Anthony Hopkins")'s *[The Father](/wiki/The_Father_(2020_film) "The Father (2020 film)")* (2020),[[29]](#cite_note-29) [Noah Baumbach](/wiki/Noah_Baumbach "Noah Baumbach")'s *[Marriage Story](/wiki/Marriage_Story "Marriage Story")* (2019),[[30]](#cite_note-30) [Peter Farrelly](/wiki/Peter_Farrelly "Peter Farrelly")'s *[Green Book](/wiki/Green_Book_(film) "Green Book (film)")* (2018),[[31]](#cite_note-31) [Luca Guadagnino](/wiki/Luca_Guadagnino "Luca Guadagnino")'s *[Call Me by Your Name](/wiki/Call_Me_by_Your_Name_(film) "Call Me by Your Name (film)")* (2017),[[32]](#cite_note-32) [Damien Chazelle](/wiki/Damien_Chazelle "Damien Chazelle")'s *[La La Land](/wiki/La_La_Land "La La Land")* (2016),[[33]](#cite_note-33) and [Adam McKay](/wiki/Adam_McKay "Adam McKay")'s *[The Big Short](/wiki/The_Big_Short_(film) "The Big Short (film)")* (2015).[[34]](#cite_note-34)

### AFI Docs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: AFI Docs")]

Held annually in June, [AFI Docs](/wiki/AFI_Docs "AFI Docs") (formerly Silverdocs) is a [documentary](/wiki/Documentary "Documentary") festival in [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.") The festival attracts over 27,000 documentary enthusiasts.

## AFI programs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: AFI programs")]

### AFI Catalog of Feature Films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: AFI Catalog of Feature Films")]

Main article: [AFI Catalog of Feature Films](/wiki/AFI_Catalog_of_Feature_Films "AFI Catalog of Feature Films")

The AFI Catalog, started in 1968, is a web-based filmographic database. A research tool for film historians, the catalog consists of entries on more than 60,000 feature films and 17,000 short films produced from 1893 to 2011, as well as AFI Awards Outstanding Movies of the Year from 2000 through 2010. Early print copies of this catalog may also be found at local libraries.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

### AFI Life Achievement Award

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: AFI Life Achievement Award")]

Main article: [AFI Life Achievement Award](/wiki/AFI_Life_Achievement_Award "AFI Life Achievement Award")

### AFI Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: AFI Awards")]

Main article: [American Film Institute Awards](/wiki/American_Film_Institute_Awards "American Film Institute Awards")

Created in 2000, the AFI Awards honor the ten outstanding films ("Movies of the Year") and ten outstanding television programs ("TV Programs of the Year").[[36]](#cite_note-36) The awards are a non-competitive acknowledgment of excellence.

The awards are announced in December, and a private luncheon for award honorees takes place the following January.

### AFI Maya Deren Award

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: AFI Maya Deren Award")]

Main article: [Maya Deren Award](/wiki/Maya_Deren_Award "Maya Deren Award")

### AFI 100 Years... series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: AFI 100 Years... series")]

The [AFI 100 Years... series](/wiki/AFI_100_Years..._series "AFI 100 Years... series"), which ran from 1998 to 2008 and created jury-selected lists of America's best movies in categories such as Musicals, Laughs and Thrills, prompted new generations to experience classic American films. The juries consisted of over 1,500 artists, scholars, critics, and historians. *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* was voted the greatest American film twice.

### AFI Silver Theatre and Cultural Center

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: AFI Silver Theatre and Cultural Center")]

The [AFI Silver](/wiki/AFI_Silver "AFI Silver") Theatre and Cultural Center is a moving image exhibition, education, and cultural center in [Silver Spring, Maryland](/wiki/Silver_Spring,_Maryland "Silver Spring, Maryland"). Its 32,000-square-foot facility, centered on architect [John Eberson](/wiki/John_Eberson "John Eberson")'s historic 1938 Silver Theatre, has two stadium theatres, office and meeting space, and reception and exhibit areas.

The AFI Silver Theatre and Cultural Center presents film and video programming, augmented by filmmaker interviews, panels, discussions, and musical performances.

### The AFI Directing Workshop for Women

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Film_Institute&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: The AFI Directing Workshop for Women")]

The [Directing Workshop for Women](/wiki/Directing_Workshop_for_Women "Directing Workshop for Women") is a training program committed to educating and mentoring participants in an effort to increase the number of women working professionally in screen directing. In this tuition-free program, each participant is required to complete a short film by the end of the year-long program.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

Alumnae of the program include [Maya Angelou](/wiki/Maya_Angelou "Maya Angelou"), [Anne Bancroft](/wiki/Anne_Bancroft "Anne Bancroft"), [Dyan Cannon](/wiki/Dyan_Cannon "Dyan Cannon"), [Ellen Burstyn](/wiki/Ellen_Burstyn "Ellen Burstyn"), [Jennifer Getzinger](/wiki/Jennifer_Getzinger "Jennifer Getzinger"), [Lesli Linka Glatter](/wiki/Lesli_Linka_Glatter "Lesli Linka Glatter"), [Lily Tomlin](/wiki/Lily_Tomlin "Lily Tomlin"), [Susan Oliver](/wiki/Susan_Oliver "Susan Oliver") and [Nancy Malone](/wiki/Nancy_Malone "Nancy Malone").[[38]](#cite_note-38)
